title: Spec Area (area | contract | ui)
description: One JSON file per area at specs/<name>.json. The formal Quint model lives in a sidecar specs/<name>.qnt referenced from formal_model.quint_file. Two kinds: 'area' (functional, default — may carry UI blocks screens/ui_components/navigation to model an interactive surface) and 'contract' (cross-area agreement, spec-only). 'ui' is a deprecated alias for area-with-screens. Most fields are optional; required fields are gated by kind or block presence. Sections grow conversationally via /spec — empty arrays/objects are valid for incomplete specs.

kind.description: area: functional area (default) — may carry UI blocks (screens/ui_components/navigation); UI-specific lint, readback, and codegen trigger on block presence, not kind. contract: cross-area invariant carrier, spec-only, requires spans[]. ui: DEPRECATED alias for area-with-screens — accepted, spec-lint warns; use kind: area with screens[] + navigation[] instead.

area.description: Slug, also the filename: specs/<area>.json. Examples: auth, billing, auth-ui, user-permission.

spans.description: Areas this spec depends on. Contracts: the 2+ areas the contract joins (required). Areas with UI blocks: the area(s) whose state the surface reads (e.g. ['auth']).

purpose.description: One paragraph: what this area is responsible for.

concepts.description: Vocabulary: entities, actors, verbs. The 'what we're talking about' layer that informs the formal model.

actors.description: Who/what can initiate actions: User, Admin, System, ScheduledJob, etc.

verbs.description: Domain actions: login, charge, expire, etc. Loose — gets refined into Quint actions during formalization.

id.description: REQ-NNN for functional behavior; UI-NNN for kind=ui; REQ-CONTRACT-NNN for kind=contract.

description.description: Human-readable rendering. When ears is present this is derived from it; the EARS fields are the source of truth.

ears.description: EARS-structured requirement (Easy Approach to Requirements Syntax). The fields ARE the structure — the EARS pattern is derived from which are filled (trigger → 'When …', state → 'While …', both → complex, feature → 'Where …'; none → ubiquitous, which belongs in invariants[] instead). Mapping to Quint: trigger → action, state → require guard, response → effect. spec-lint validates; a requirement without ears past status raw/needs-validation gets a WARN.

state.description: Precondition / system mode ('While ...'). Maps to a Quint require guard.

trigger.description: Initiating event ('When/If ...'). Maps to a Quint action. Required when unwanted is true.

unwanted.description: True when this is error/abnormal-situation handling ('If <trigger>, then …'). The one EARS distinction not derivable from field presence.

response.description: What the system shall do. Maps to the Quint action's effect.

witness.description: Witness obligation: machine-found proof that this behavior is reachable in the model. /spec-check negates the predicate, runs the checker, and saves the counterexample (= the witness trace) as ITF. status 'no-witness' means the behavior is UNREACHABLE — a vacuity red flag. spec-lint blocks approval while any requirement is unwitnessed.

predicate.description: Quint boolean expression (or val name) over state that is true exactly when the required behavior has happened. Example for 'account locks after N failures': accountStatus.keys().exists(u => accountStatus.get(u) == Locked).

trace.description: ITF trace path relative to specs/, e.g. 'auth/traces/REQ-003.itf.json'. Written by /spec-check.

justification.description: Required when status is 'skipped'. Typical case: a rejection requirement ('shall be rejected') has no state change to witness — pair it with the invariant that stays true and point here, e.g. 'rejection — enforced by INV-002 noSessionWhileLocked'.

model_sha.description: sha256 of the model the trace was found against (.qnt + .probes.qnt; see `tools/itf_tools.py sha <area>`). spec-lint FAILs when this no longer matches — a trace from an older model proves nothing about the current one.

quint_ref.description: Name of the Quint action that realizes this requirement.

quint_name.description: Name of the Quint `val` or `invariant` that encodes this.

name.description: Symbolic name used in code, e.g. MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS.

decisions.description: ADRs. kind='architecture' entries pair with the architecture section to record WHY non-obvious choices were made.

state_machines.description: Explicit state machines for stateful entities. Optional but valuable: spec-lint validates structural completeness (unreachable states, dangling non-terminal states, transitions referencing undeclared states, Quint mutations not listed as transitions) without needing Apalache. /spec-readback renders the diagram from this section. Each entry corresponds to one entity from concepts.entities[] that has states[].

entity.description: Entity name. Must match a concepts.entities[].name.

quint_var.description: Quint var that holds this state in the sidecar (used by spec-lint to cross-check).

quint_type.description: Quint variant type defining the state values.

initial_state.description: The state the entity is in after `init`. Must appear in states[].

name.description: State name; matches a variant constructor in quint_type.

terminal.description: No outgoing transitions expected. Lint warns if a terminal state has any.

lifecycle_actions.description: Quint actions that mutate quint_var but aren't state transitions on an existing instance — creation, destruction, garbage collection. Suppresses the 'sidecar action mutates quint_var but isn't listed as a transition' warning for these. Example: login creates a Session in initial_state, so it goes here, not in transitions[].

from.description: Origin state (must be in states[]) or '*' meaning 'from any non-terminal state'.

trigger.description: Action or event name; usually matches a Quint action.

actor.description: Who or what initiates the transition: User, Admin, System, etc.

guard.description: Plain-language precondition. The Quint action encodes the formal version.

quint_action.description: Name of the Quint action that realizes this transition. spec-lint checks the sidecar mutation of quint_var matches the transitions listed here.

architecture.description: Per-area architecture (Layer 0-6 except diagrams). Sparse: only declares overrides from project defaults in .spec/project.json. inherits_project=true (default) merges; false makes this fully standalone.

implements.description: Quint action names this component implements.

architecture_file.description: Optional sidecar specs/<area>/components/<name>.json with a fuller per-component architecture.

module_layout.description: Path templates. Variables: {area}, {component}, {ext}. Paths relative to code_root resolved from .spec/project.json.

cross_cutting.description: logging, metrics, tracing, auth, feature_flags. Each is a string or object.

quint_file.description: Sidecar path, e.g. 'auth.qnt' (relative to this JSON). The Quint module is the source of truth for the formal spec; module name and imports are read from it directly via tools/quint_ir.py — never mirrored here.

probes_file.description: Generated probe module, e.g. 'auth.probes.qnt' (relative to this JSON). Contains the ghost-instrumented step wrapper and the path-constrained witness probes. Regenerated by /spec-check; never hand-edited.

conformance.description: Model-based conformance testing config: how witness traces are replayed against real code. /spec-apply generates the adapter and harness; /spec-verify runs the command and treats a requirement as verified only when its witness trace replays green.

adapter.description: Path (relative to code root) of the adapter mapping Quint actions to code API calls and Quint vars to observable code state.

harness.description: Path (relative to tests root) of the trace-replay harness that loads ITF files and drives the adapter.

command.description: Shell command that replays all traces, e.g. 'pnpm test conformance'. Run from the code repo root by /spec-verify.

traces_dir.description: Where witness traces live, relative to specs/. Default: '<area>/traces'.

traceability.description: Maps each requirement/invariant to its Quint construct, the component implementing it, and the code/test locations. Filled in by /spec-apply.

screens.description: Optional on any area. Each screen is a top-level navigable view. Declaring screens[] + navigation[] makes the area an interactive surface: navigation lint, navigation-graph readback, and component codegen activate.

components.description: Names from ui_components[] that appear on this screen.

states.description: Visible states: idle, loading, error, etc.

visible_when.description: Plain-language condition or Quint predicate name.

navigation.description: Allowed transitions between screens (areas with screens). Required when screens[] is declared.

trigger.description: Plain-language user action: 'click "Sign in"', 'submit valid credentials'.

guard.description: Optional precondition referencing a Quint val or another area's state.

conformance.description: Trace-replay results from this run (see conformance section).

check_results.description: Latest Apalache results. Overwritten by /spec-check each run.

id.description: INV-NNN or PROP-NNN. Witness results live in requirements[].witness, not here.

trace.description: Counterexample ITF path relative to specs/, if any.

counterexample.description: NL explanation of the counterexample (nl_explanation). The raw trace lives in the file at `trace`, not inline.
